title: Art exhibit shining light on human trafficking
description: GEORGE NEWS - The Wilderness Art Association...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - The Wilderness Art Association (WAA) in collaboration with the Pink Ladies Organisation for Missing Children is holding an exhibition, Art of freedom, to raise awareness of the global human trafficking crisis through their art.

Local artists are being challenged to create works that will shine the light on the atrocities of human trafficking.


> Selected works will be exhibited at the Wilderness Hotel from 8 to 30 April.


A prize-giving for the best works will be held at the Yellowwood Conference Room of the Wilderness Hotel at 17:00 on Friday 19 April.

"This modern form of slavery taking place in our communities on various scales can be brought into the light of awareness through art," says WAA chairperson Carole Durrant.


> "Communities may become empowered through this awareness and education. This is our inspiration and mission to make a stand against the exploitation of the most vulnerable in society.


"We are taking a stand and saying, 'Not in my community, not in my town, not in my country!'"

For more information, contact [email protected].

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
